content:
You are a security triage assistant for Rocky/RHEL 9 OpenSCAP STIG findings.
Return ONLY a single JSON object that matches the schema exactly.
No prose. No markdown. No code fences. No extra keys.
Be conservative: if uncertain, choose requires_human_review.

Classify this OpenSCAP finding into exactly one category:
safe_to_remediate, requires_human_review, too_dangerous_to_remediate.

Return ONLY JSON for this schema:
{
  "finding_id": string,
  "rule_id": string,
  "category": "safe_to_remediate"|"requires_human_review"|"too_dangerous_to_remediate",
  "confidence": number between 0 and 1,
  "rationale": string,
  "risk_factors": [string,...],
  "safe_next_steps": [string,...],
  "requires_reboot": boolean,
  "touches_authn_authz": boolean,
  "touches_networking": boolean,
  "touches_filesystems": boolean
}

Finding:
- finding_id: openscap_063
- rule_id: A remote time server for Chrony is configured
- severity: 2
- title: A remote time server for Chrony is configured
- description: Chrony is a daemon which implements the Network Time Protocol (NTP). It is designed to synchronize system clocks across a variety of systems and use a source that is highly accurate. More information on chrony can be found at https://chrony-project.org/. Chrony can be configured to be a client and/or a server. Add or edit server or pool lines to /etc/chrony.conf as appropriate: server <remote-server> Multiple servers may be configured.
- recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q chrony; }; then var_multiple_time_servers='' config_file="/etc/chrony.conf" if ! grep -q '^[[:space:]]*\(server\|pool\)[[:space:]]\+[[:graph:]]\+' "$config_file" ; then if ! grep -q '#[[:space:]]*server' "$config_file" ; then for server in $(echo "$var_multiple_time_servers" | tr ',' '\n') ; do printf '\nserver %s' "$server" >> "$config_file" done else sed -i 's/#[ \t]*server/server/g' "$config_file" fi if [[ -s "$config_file" ]] && [[ -n "$(tail -c 1 -- "$config_file" || true)" ]]; then LC_ALL=C sed -i --follow-symlinks '$a'\\ "$config_file" fi fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

Policy:
- Be conservative. If unclear, choose requires_human_review.
- Mark too_dangerous_to_remediate for partitioning/filesystem/bootloader/FIPS changes.
- Mark requires_human_review for auth/ssh/sudo/pam changes that could lock out access, EXCEPT password-related rules.
- Mark safe_to_remediate for password policy/complexity/expiration/history rules (pwquality, chage, password length, password age, password complexity, lockout, etc.).
- Mark safe_to_remediate for low-risk package installs, service enablement, sysctl persistence that is unlikely to lock out access.

{
  "finding_id": "openscap_063",
  "rule_id": "A remote time server for Chrony is configured",
  "category": "requires_human_review",
  "confidence": 0.8,
  "rationale": "The remediation script modifies the /etc/chrony.conf file, which could potentially impact system time synchronization and overall system stability.",
  "risk_factors": ["system stability", "time synchronization"],
  "safe_next_steps": ["review the current Chrony configuration", "test the remediation script in a non-production environment"],
  "requires_reboot": false,
  "touches_authn_authz": false,
  "touches_networking": true,
  "touches_filesystems": true
}
